methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Limelight Steel is a pre-seed-stage climate and energy technology company led by founder Olivia Dippo. The company has raised $6M to date. While 21 private peers operate within the Climate & Energy sector, the peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient comparables at the same or adjacent stage with similar valuation tier characteristics—no narrow peer set of three or more companies met the filtering criteria.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $6M as a base proxy and applying a typical pre-seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $15M. This single-method triangulation constrains the range between $13M (low, representing a 2.125x multiple) and $17M (high, representing a 2.875x multiple), with $15M as the median point.

The analysis carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method and the absence of stage-specific peer validation. Limelight Steel's valuation is most defensible in the $13M to $17M range, centered at $15M, pending availability of tighter comparables or additional financial data points. Key variables affecting forward valuation include technology differentiation and capital efficiency relative to the broader frontier-energy cohort.
